title: Chicken-and-Egg Bypass Design

content.question: What artificial or manual moves will you make pre-launch to make your business feel alive from day one?
content.equation: CEB = (Synthetic Liquidity × Experience Quality) ÷ Cost of Support
sop: List the manual or unscalable moves you could make to simulate activity before real supply or demand exists.
Rate the Synthetic Liquidity 1-10, how convincingly those moves create the feeling of an active market (see calculation, page 6).
Rate the Experience Quality 1-10, whether early users get a genuinely good experience, not just a busy-looking one (see calculation, page 6).
Rate the Cost of Support 1-10, how expensive or time-consuming those manual moves are to sustain (see calculation, page 6).
Divide the product of Synthetic Liquidity and Experience Quality by Cost of Support and commit to your manual bootstrap only as long as the ratio stays favorable.
content.explainer: Every great marketplace faked it before it made it. The trick is the fake feels real to the first user.
questions: What artificial or manual moves will you make pre-launch to make your business feel alive from day one?
If your first 20 users showed up today, would there be enough real activity for them to see, or would it look empty?
What are you personally willing to do manually, deliver it yourself, fake the supply, seed the reviews, to make the first experience feel real?

definitions.def: The problem of getting a marketplace started when neither side will join until the other side is there.
In a marketplace, how fast a user gets what they came for. Slow matching = pre-value churn. Speed of satisfaction is the metric.

content.equation_example: Rate each 1-10. Synthetic Liquidity 7, Experience Quality 8. 7 times 8 equals 56, divided by Cost of Support 5 equals 11.2. Higher means your manual bootstrap creates a real, good experience without draining you. Lower means you're either faking it unconvincingly or burning yourself out to keep the illusion alive.
content.closing_statement: Early momentum can be manufactured and still feel real.

caption:
Principle Nº 76: Chicken-and-Egg Bypass Design

Early-stage networks require artificial liquidity. Every great marketplace faked it before it made it.

Ask yourself: What artificial or manual moves will you make pre-launch to make your business feel alive from day one?
